practices.title: Call tools, don't reason
practices.tagline: Validated tools replace guesswork. The LLM orchestrates; the tool decides.
practices.beforeExample:
User: Is this function O(n²) or O(n log n)?
LLM: [reasons through the code, may be wrong]
practices.restricts: No free-form reasoning that invents facts.
practices.quality: Ground truth injected by the tool becomes a constraint in subsequent turns.

practices.title: RAG for canonical docs
practices.tagline: Fetch specs on demand. Never paste a 3k-token OpenAPI into a prompt.
practices.beforeExample:
User: Here's our API spec [3000 tokens of OpenAPI].
  Add a users endpoint.
practices.afterExample:
User: Add a users endpoint per the API spec index.
LLM: → rag.search("users endpoint conventions")
  pulls 400 relevant tokens. Cites chunk IDs.
practices.reinforces: The canonical spec is the source of truth, and it stays up to date.
practices.restricts: No drift — all ten agents reading the same index see the same rules.
practices.quality: Context budget preserved for the current turn; retrieval is precise.

practices.title: Memory files for sticky state
practices.tagline: MEMORY.md carries decisions across sessions without bloating context.
practices.beforeExample:
Every new session: re-explain the whole project.
The model re-learns the same facts 40 times.
practices.afterExample:
MEMORY.md pinned at session start:
  - We use Postgres 15, not Mongo.
  - HARD: 90-day retention.
  - JWT is RS256, never HS256.
practices.restricts: Model can't re-invent architectural choices each session.
practices.quality: Consistency across sessions costs ~200 tokens, not ~20k.

practices.title: Clear context after scoped work
practices.tagline: Finish a task → new chat. Don't let sessions metastasize.
practices.beforeExample:
Same chat for 3 days. Features, bugs, architecture, billing.
  Turn 60: constraint from turn 3 is long forgotten.
practices.afterExample:
One chat per concern. When done:
  - Summarize decisions into MEMORY.md
  - Start fresh chat for next concern
practices.reinforces: Critical invariants stay in the recency window.
practices.quality: Each session operates at peak recall for its scope.

practices.title: Declare scope upfront
practices.tagline: State the boundary at turn 1. Refuse to wander.
practices.beforeExample:
User: Fix this billing bug.
[20 turns later] User: Also add a new feature.
LLM: [context now covers both, constraints from each dilute each other]
practices.afterExample:
User: SCOPE: billing bug only. Out of scope:
  new features, refactors, unrelated cleanup.
[new concern appears] → user starts a new chat
practices.reinforces: The boundary itself is a constraint the model checks against.
practices.restricts: No scope creep → no cross-contamination of concerns.
practices.quality: Focused sessions produce focused, testable deliverables.

practices.title: Summarize & prune history
practices.tagline: Compress aged turns. Keep the decisions, drop the chatter.
practices.beforeExample:
Turn 1–25 verbatim in every subsequent prompt.
  90% of context is "yes, let me try" and "hmm, that didn't work."
practices.afterExample:
Every N turns, summarize:
  [SUMMARY of turns 1–25]
  - Decided: Postgres 15, RS256 JWT, 90d retention.
  - Rejected: Mongo (licensing), HS256 (rotation).
Then drop the verbatim turns.
practices.reinforces: Key decisions surface to the recency window.
practices.quality: Context stays dense; attention stays focused.

practices.title: Input guardrails
practices.tagline: Validate what enters the context before the model sees it.
practices.beforeExample:
User pastes CSV with real names, SSNs, emails.
LLM: [reads PII, includes in generated code samples]
practices.afterExample:
Pipe user input through pii_redactor first.
Only redacted content reaches the prompt.
Policy-checked before assembly.
practices.reinforces: Compliance is a property of the context, not a hope.
practices.restricts: Governance violations can't even be attempted.
practices.quality: Context is safe to log, cache, and replay.

practices.title: Output schemas, not prose
practices.tagline: Structured returns. A validator rejects what doesn't match.
practices.beforeExample:
LLM returns: "I think we should probably use..."
Caller: parses free-form prose, hopes for the best.
practices.afterExample:
Prompt: return as { decision, reason, confidence, alternatives }
Validator rejects malformed returns → LLM retries.
practices.reinforces: The shape of correct output is unambiguous.
practices.restricts: No hedging prose; no creative alternate formats.
practices.quality: Downstream systems consume structured data reliably.

practices.title: MUST / MUST NOT lists
practices.tagline: Hard rules as enumerated bullets, not prose.
practices.beforeExample:
Please try to make sure the code handles edge cases,
  and preferably uses our coding style.
practices.afterExample:
MUST:
  - Return { ok, data, error } shape
  - Idempotent on (source, external_id)
MUST NOT:
  - Use console.log
  - Call external APIs without retry
practices.restricts: Prose hedges disappear; model is less tempted to "interpret."
practices.quality: Violation is detectable, automatable, and fixable in one pass.

practices.title: Cite your grounding
practices.tagline: Every decision traces back to a spec section or retrieved chunk.
practices.beforeExample:
LLM: We'll use AES-256 because it's industry standard.
[no trace — could be invented]
practices.afterExample:
LLM: AES-256 per security-spec.md §4.2 (retrieved).
Schema length 80 per data-model.md §schema.email.
[Every decision has a source tag]
practices.reinforces: Every output token is traceable to a constraint or fact.
practices.restricts: Confabulation has nowhere to hide — unsourced claims stand out.
practices.quality: Review becomes "verify the citations," not "re-derive everything."
